rulings.Case Title: SOTO VS LI
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Dismiss Plaintiffs Action; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities; Declaration Of Milan Yancich (Proposed)Order
rulings.Rulings: MOTION TO DISMISS DENIED. PLAINTIFF HAS SHOWN GOOD CAUSE FOR FAILURE TO SERVE WITHIN TWO YEARS. (302/AJR/KS(EX))

rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Settlement & Motion For Determination Of Good Faith Settlement; (2)Declarations In Support
rulings.Rulings: HEARING REQUIRED. (302/AJR/PB)

rulings.Case Title: SMITH VS KNOLLER
rulings.Calendar Matter: To File Motion For Good Faith Settlement Under Seal
rulings.Rulings: HEARING REQUIRED. MOVING PARTY TO ADDRESS: (1)WHETHER MOTION WAS SERVED ON CO-DEFENDANTS, SEE MEDIPLEX OF CA, INC., V. SUPERIOR COURT, (1995) 34 CAL.APP.4TH 748; (2)WHETHER OVERRIDING INTEREST OF ENFORCING BINDING CONTRACT OBLIGATIONS APPLIES TO PUTATIVE AGREEMENTS; (3)WHETHER MOVING PARTY ONLY WANTS AMOUNT SEALED; (4)WHAT DECLARATIONS, IF ANY, SUPPORT MOTION TO SEAL. (302/AJR/PB)

rulings.Case Title: EDYTHE WHIPPLE-KELLY VS ROBERT NOEL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion To File Motion For Good Faith Settlement Under Seal
rulings.Rulings: HEARING REQUIRED. MOVING PARTY TO ADDRESS: (1)WHETHER MOTION WAS SERVED ON CO-DEFENDANTS, SEE MEDIPLEX OF CA, INC., V. SUPERIOR COURT, (1995) 34 CAL.APP.4TH 748; (2)WHETHER OVERRIDING INTEREST OF ENFORCING BINDING CONTRACT OBLIGATIONS APPLIES TO PUTATIVE AGREEMENTS; (3)WHETHER MOVING PARTY ONLY WANTS AMOUNT SEALED; (4)WHAT DECLARATIONS, IF ANY, SUPPORT MOTION TO SEAL. (302/AJR/PB)

rulings.Case Title: CONSUMER ADVOCACY VS FRANCISCO BAY INN
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn & Mtn For Judicial Approval Of Settlement Of Action Between Consumer Advocacy Group Inc & Comfort Inn Airport West
rulings.Rulings: GRANT. (302/AJR/ju)

rulings.Case Title: HOUSTON VS RABB
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION Notice Of Motion And Motion To Set Aside The Default And Default Judgment;
rulings.Rulings: MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT AND DEFAULT JUDGMENT DENIED. MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT UNTIMELY IN THAT MOTION NOT MADE UNTIL AFTER 6 MONTHS AFTER ENTRY OF DEFAULT. INADEQUATE SHOWING OF LACK OF SERVICE. NO ADEQUATE SEPARATE REASON. ADVANCED FOR VACATION OF DEFAULT JUDGMENT SEPARATELY. (302/AJR/KS(EX))

rulings.Case Title: BERGERON VS LEE
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn & Mtn For Order Determining Good Faith Settlement
rulings.Rulings: DENY WITHOUT PREJUDICE. SEE MATTCO FORGE, INC. V. ARTHUR YOUNG & CO., (1995) 38 CAL.APP.4TH 1337, 1350, FN.6; MEDIPLEX OF CALIFORNIA, INC. V. SUPERIOR COURT, (1995) 34 CAL.APP.4TH 748. (302/AJR/PB)
